headline: China's ByteDance gets access to top Nvidia AI chips, WSJ reports
summary: TikTok's Chinese parent ByteDance is assembling computing power with top Nvidia chips ‌outside China, the Wall Street Journal reported ‌on Thursday.  ByteDance is working with Southeast Asian firm Aolani Cloud to ​deploy about 500 Nvidia Blackwell computing systems in Malaysia, totaling roughly 36,000 B200 chips, the WSJ report said, citing people familiar with the matter.  Aolani acquires these ‌servers from Aivres, ⁠a company that assembles servers using Nvidia chips, the report said, adding that ⁠if the plan goes through, the hardware involved could cost more than $2.5 billion.
